120b5168-4b2c-441d-ac0b-dae4abee30ff.title: Quality pass: clippy pedantic cleanup in crossbridge-e2e
120b5168-4b2c-441d-ac0b-dae4abee30ff.description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

Run cargo clippy -p crossbridge-e2e with -W clippy::pedantic and resolve all 57 warnings. Top categories: missing_errors_doc, cast_possible_truncation, map_unwrap_or, similar_names (labels_a_after vs labels_b_after — keep them, allow similar_names for the e2e test specifically), too_many_lines (the round_trip test is intentionally a single linear narrative — allow too_many_lines on that fn with a comment), manual_assert.

Acceptance criteria:
- cargo clippy -p crossbridge-e2e --all-targets -- -W clippy::pedantic -D warnings exits 0.
- No blanket #[allow] over whole modules — fix the underlying issues; only allow specific lints with a one-line justification.
- The submit_then_answer_round_trip integration test must still run unconditionally (no #[ignore]) and pass.
- cargo fmt --check clean.

1ad3368f-d913-4ec0-9a63-c938204e217f.title: Quality pass: clippy pedantic cleanup in crossbridge-protocol
1ad3368f-d913-4ec0-9a63-c938204e217f.description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

Run `cargo clippy -p crossbridge-protocol --all-targets -- -W clippy::pedantic` and resolve every warning (currently 10). Top categories: cast_possible_truncation, missing_errors_doc.

Acceptance criteria:
- `cargo clippy -p crossbridge-protocol --all-targets -- -W clippy::pedantic -D warnings` exits 0.
- No blanket `#[allow(clippy::pedantic)]` or `#[allow(...)]` over whole modules — fix the underlying issues, only allow specific lints with a one-line justification comment when the pedantic suggestion is genuinely worse.
- All existing tests still pass (`cargo test -p crossbridge-protocol`).
- `cargo fmt --check` clean.
- Cast-truncation warnings: prefer `u32::try_from(...)?` over `as u32` at wire-protocol boundaries; use `#[allow(clippy::cast_possible_truncation)]` only where bounds are statically guaranteed (with a comment).

1b7bc2e1-2f14-4c9b-bdd6-4efe04a88ad6.title: Drop Determinate Systems magic-nix-cache from CI
1b7bc2e1-2f14-4c9b-bdd6-4efe04a88ad6.description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

Magic Nix Cache is deprecated (stops working Feb 2026), and the successor FlakeHub Cache requires Determinate Systems auth — overkill for a personal project. cache.nixos.org is already a fast binary cache, and Swatinem/rust-cache covers cargo target. Drop the magic-nix-cache step. Cost: ~30-60s extra per job pulling rustc/clippy/etc. fresh from cache.nixos.org. Acceptable for a personal project.

AC:
- .github/workflows/ci.yml no longer references DeterminateSystems/magic-nix-cache-action.
- Resulting CI run on main passes all three jobs (test, clippy, fmt).

1bfe6fbb-416e-4431-843d-d2bf75b4138c.title: Add GitHub Actions CI for tests, clippy pedantic, and fmt
1bfe6fbb-416e-4431-843d-d2bf75b4138c.description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

No CI runs against this repo today. Local enforcement happens via .crosslink/hook-config.json during kickoffs but humans pushing directly bypass it. Pedantic-clean state and 92 passing tests will silently rot.

Add .github/workflows/ci.yml that runs on push to main and on every pull_request.

Acceptance criteria:
- File exists at .github/workflows/ci.yml.
- Three jobs (or one job with three steps; matrix not required): test, clippy, fmt.
- test: cargo test --workspace exits 0.
- clippy: cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -W clippy::pedantic -D warnings exits 0. (Match the AC we just landed for #33.)
- fmt: cargo fmt --check exits 0.
- Toolchain version matches workspace rust-version (1.87) — pin via dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable with toolchain: '1.87' or via rust-toolchain.toml file.
- Cargo registry + target dir cached for speed (Swatinem/rust-cache@v2 is the standard).
- ubuntu-latest runner.
- Triggers: pull_request (any branch), push (main only).
- Workflow name in GitHub UI is 'CI'.
- README gets a CI badge linking to the workflow runs page.
- The first run of the workflow on this branch must pass — verify after pushing the branch.

Out of scope:
- nix-build / nixos test integration (separate concern).
- Coverage / codecov.
- Release automation, crate publishing.
- Cross-platform matrix.
- Caching beyond rust-cache.

248b66ed-426d-4d68-97f3-74328bcc5a6a.title: End-to-end integration test + unify CROSSBRIDGE_SOCKET_ROOT across all three binaries
248b66ed-426d-4d68-97f3-74328bcc5a6a.description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

Add a workspace-level integration test that wires up real crossbridge-supervisor + two real crossbridge-server instances + the real crossbridge-client binary to verify the full bidirectional flow: an issue submitted from repo-a's agent reaches repo-b's crosslink DB as an inbound issue, and an answer issued from repo-b's agent flows back to close out repo-a's outbound issue with the result comments attached.

Existing per-crate integration tests each stub out the components on the other side of their crate boundary (the client tests use a thread-bound mock server; the server's E2E test uses a fake supervisor and a raw socket client; the supervisor tests are pure topology). None exercise the full topology, and none verify that xb-status:answered on the responder lines up with xb-status:resolved + closure on the originator across two separate crosslink databases.

Two production-code prerequisites first:

1. Unify the runtime-root override on a single env var, CROSSBRIDGE_SOCKET_ROOT, in all three binaries. Today the client reads it; the supervisor only takes --register-socket; the server only takes --runtime-root. Make all three honor the env var (precedence: CLI flag > env var > compiled-in default '/run/crossbridge'). Export the env-var name as a public constant in crossbridge-protocol and have crossbridge-client re-export it.

2. Then add a new workspace member crate 'crossbridge-e2e' at the project root with tests/round_trip.rs that drives real binaries via CARGO_BIN_EXE_* against a single tempdir runtime root configured via the unified env var.

Full spec, acceptance criteria, scenario, and constraints in .design/e2e-integration-test.md (committed alongside this issue).

2cb00705-b1b3-4929-9722-d8bbf56bbe09.title: Package crossbridge as systemd service for vringar/nixos-setup

94849f1c-de9b-47d7-b664-6ea4192e530d.author: 2ZD1
94849f1c-de9b-47d7-b664-6ea4192e530d.content: Crossbridge needs to be packaged as a Nix flake output (binary + systemd unit) so vringar/nixos-setup can import it. Requires: (1) flake.nix with package output, (2) a NixOS module with systemd timer+service, (3) config path option pointing to the per-machine crossbridge.toml.

d6436924-91dd-4c9a-b04e-c796829af2b8.author: 2ZD1
d6436924-91dd-4c9a-b04e-c796829af2b8.content: Created default.nix (callPackage derivation with rustPlatform.buildRustPackage) and nix/module.nix (NixOS module with systemd timer+service, DynamicUser, configurable interval/logLevel)

ea3de800-41b8-49c9-93aa-c3eba6d5d0e0.author: 2ZD1
ea3de800-41b8-49c9-93aa-c3eba6d5d0e0.content: Design decision: NO flake. Use a traditional default.nix (callPackage pattern) so vringar/nixos-setup can import directly and pass in the correct crosslink source/version at build time. Module provides: services.crossbridge.{enable, configFile, interval}. Package output includes the binary + wrapper scripts in bin/.

3fda75ff-7247-4989-b69d-06ecf0bbbc74.title: Harden crossbridge-server: close inherited file descriptors at startup
3fda75ff-7247-4989-b69d-06ecf0bbbc74.description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

## Context

The direnv helper (`nix/direnvrc.sh`) launches `crossbridge-server` detached. A
bug there leaked direnv's `.envrc` evaluation pipe (fd 3) into the long-lived
server: direnv never saw EOF on that pipe, so the shell that triggered `.envrc`
hung until the server exited. Fixed launcher-side by adding `3>&-` to the
detached launch.

That fix is launcher-specific. This issue is the defense-in-depth complement:
make `crossbridge-server` itself drop any inherited file descriptors at startup,
so it is robust against *any* launcher (Makefile, CI wrapper, shell script, ...)
that leaks an fd.

## Why this is safe for this server

`crossbridge-server` never legitimately inherits a file descriptor:

- It **connects out** to the supervisor (`UnixStream::connect`, `supervisor.rs`).
- It **creates** its own per-peer listening sockets (`listeners.rs`).
- It opens its own crosslink DB, tokio epoll fd, and log sink.
- `.design/overview.md`: the server is "started manually in tmux (not systemd)"
  — no socket activation, no fd-passing anywhere in the design.

Every fd >= 3 the process starts with is therefore a launcher leak.

## What to implement

In `crossbridge-server/src/main.rs`, as the **first statement of `main()`** —
before `tracing_subscriber`, before `Cli::parse()`, before the tokio runtime
builds its epoll fd — close every inherited fd >= 3:

```rust
// Drop every inherited fd >= 3 before we open any of our own. A launcher
// (e.g. a direnv .envrc) may leak its evaluation pipe to us; holding it
// open hangs the launcher until this long-lived process exits.
```

- Add `libc` as a direct dependency of `crossbridge-server` (already transitive
  via tokio; one Cargo.toml line, no compile cost).
- Use `close_range(2)` — a single atomic syscall, no iteration races. It
  requires Linux 5.9+. If `close_range` returns `ENOSYS`, fall back to
  iterating `/proc/self/fd` and closing each entry >= 3.
- Leave fds 0/1/2 untouched — fd 1/2 carry `tracing` output; fd 0 is always
  valid as launched.

## Caveat to document in the code comment

Closing inherited fds would break systemd socket activation (which passes
sockets starting at fd 3 with `$LISTEN_FDS` set). The server has no
socket-activation path today, but the comment must call this out so a future
change is aware. Optional: skip the close when `$LISTEN_FDS` is set.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `crossbridge-server` closes all inherited fds >= 3 as the first action in `main()`.
- [ ] Implemented with `close_range`, with a `/proc/self/fd` fallback on `ENOSYS`.
- [ ] fds 0/1/2 are left intact.
- [ ] A test verifies an fd opened before `exec` of the server is not held open
      by the running server (e.g. spawn the server with an extra inherited pipe
      fd, assert the write end reaches EOF / the fd is absent from
      `/proc/<pid>/fd`).
- [ ] `cargo test`, `cargo clippy`, `cargo fmt` all clean.
- [ ] The `3>&-` fix in `nix/direnvrc.sh` stays — this is complementary, not a replacement.

427dd451-cf9d-48a7-a3b7-93c0565ad214.title: Convert to Cargo workspace, implement shared protocol crate with postcard message types and framing helpers

1f4b9801-b6d5-4497-ac9c-8ed0836b1a35.author: va8e
1f4b9801-b6d5-4497-ac9c-8ed0836b1a35.content: Plan: Convert single-crate project to Cargo workspace with two members: (1) crates/crossbridge/ for the existing CLI binary, (2) crates/crossbridge-protocol/ for the new shared protocol crate. The protocol crate will define message types (Request, Response, StatusUpdate, Error) using serde + postcard serialization, plus length-prefix framing helpers for encode/decode over streams. Will keep it no_std-compatible with alloc where possible.

4ea25389-2318-4afd-9859-2f770f006b1b.content: Plan: Protocol crate defines: Message enum (Request/Response/StatusUpdate variants), Priority enum, Status enum, Request struct (source, target, title, body, priority, uuid), Response struct (source_uuid, target_uuid, body), StatusUpdate struct (uuid, old/new status). Framing uses u32 LE length prefix + postcard serialization. decode_frame reads from byte slice returning (Message, bytes_consumed). All types derive Serialize/Deserialize/Debug/Clone/PartialEq.

53d00b7b-9953-491c-8a26-e0ea988a2ca8.title: Quality pass: clippy pedantic clean workspace-wide
53d00b7b-9953-491c-8a26-e0ea988a2ca8.description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

Resolve every pedantic warning across the entire workspace in one coherent pass.

Why workspace-wide and not per-crate: `cargo clippy -p <X> -- -W clippy::pedantic -D warnings` lints the entire dep graph, so a per-crate AC isn't independently verifiable until every upstream is also clean. The previous attempt (issues #18-#23, now closed as superseded) coordinated badly across six parallel kickoffs and was abandoned.

Acceptance criteria:
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -W clippy::pedantic -D warnings` exits 0.
- No blanket #[allow(clippy::pedantic)] over whole modules. Specific-lint allows must have a one-line justification comment when the pedantic suggestion is genuinely worse (e.g. similar_names on test variables that are intentionally parallel; too_many_lines on a deliberately linear narrative test; unused_async on a helper that hosts a future without a direct .await).
- All existing tests still pass: `cargo test --workspace` (currently 92 passed, 0 ignored, 0 failed).
- `cargo fmt --check` clean.
- Wire protocol behavior, public APIs, and observable test outcomes are unchanged. This is a quality pass, not a refactor — do not change message types, label schemes, or observable behavior.
- For cast-truncation lints (cast_possible_truncation, cast_sign_loss): prefer u32::try_from(...)? at boundaries; allow only where bounds are statically guaranteed (with a comment).

Reference categories from the orchestrator's pre-cleanup run:
- doc_markdown (~25 sites: missing backticks around type names in doc comments)
- cast_possible_truncation (~16)
- missing_errors_doc (~10)
- map_unwrap_or, manual_map (~5)
- unnecessary_wraps (jj_origin_url returns Result<Option<String>> but never Errs)
- needless_pass_by_value, too_many_lines, similar_names, manual_assert (a handful each)

Total roughly 168 pedantic warnings across the workspace as of the last clean main.

5e7ba92f-f72e-440b-a9f3-eb3ee122ba2b.description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

First CI run on main (run 25601663135) failed because transitive deps now require rustc 1.88+: darling 0.23, instability 0.3.12, time 0.3.47. Locally rustc 1.94 from the nix shell hides this. Bump workspace rust-version to 1.88 and switch CI toolchain to 'stable' (drop the explicit 1.87 pin).

63e37641-f3e2-43e3-8e8a-8a55fab810f4.title: Remove legacy v1 crossbridge polling crate
63e37641-f3e2-43e3-8e8a-8a55fab810f4.description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

## Problem

The original polling-cycle binary in `crossbridge/` predates the v2
supervisor + server + client architecture and is no longer part of the
shipped design. README explicitly calls it "legacy ... not part of the new
architecture", but it is still:

- a workspace member in `Cargo.toml`,
- built and installed by `default.nix`, and
- declared as `mainProgram = "crossbridge"` in `default.nix:29` — so
  `nix run` invokes the deprecated binary.

The v1 helper scripts `script/crossbridge-request` / `script/crossbridge-answer`
only emit v1 labels (`xb-target:`, `xb-status:open`, `xb-status:answered`)
that nothing in v2 consumes. They are still installed by `postInstall` in
`default.nix:22-23`.

Keeping the legacy crate around silently steers users to the wrong tool and
forces v2 docs/skill to keep distinguishing "legacy vs current".

## Scope

1. Remove the `crossbridge/` crate:
   - Delete the directory.
   - Drop `"crossbridge"` from `[workspace] members` in the root `Cargo.toml`.
   - Regenerate `Cargo.lock`.
2. Update `default.nix`:
   - Drop the `postInstall` lines installing `crossbridge-request` /
     `crossbridge-answer`.
   - Change `mainProgram` to one of the v2 binaries
     (`crossbridge-supervisor` is the most natural — it is what the systemd
     module wraps).
3. Delete `script/crossbridge-request` and `script/crossbridge-answer`
   (they encode the v1 label protocol; v2 uses `crossbridge-client`).
4. README cleanup:
   - Drop the "legacy" bullet under "The package installs" (`README.md:104-105`).
   - Drop the row for `crossbridge/` in the Repo layout table.
   - Remove any other references to the legacy binary or helper scripts.
5. CHANGELOG: add an `### Removed` entry under `[Unreleased]`.

## Preconditions

- The agent skill update (companion issue) should land first or in the same
  change, otherwise the skill is left pointing at scripts that no longer
  exist.

## Acceptance

- `cargo build --workspace` and `cargo test --workspace` succeed.
- `nix-build` produces a derivation whose `bin/` contains only the v2
  binaries (`crossbridge-supervisor`, `crossbridge-server`,
  `crossbridge-client`).
- `rg -i "legacy|crossbridge-request|crossbridge-answer"` returns no hits in
  user-facing files (README, skill, design docs).
- No remaining references to the v1 polling protocol in shipped code or docs.

1bcfa79a-874f-4d7e-a3df-b8a23eebcc34.author: 2ZD1
1bcfa79a-874f-4d7e-a3df-b8a23eebcc34.content: Landed via cherry-pick onto main as 868e65c. Took the v1 crate removal, default.nix mainProgram fix, README/CHANGELOG cleanup, and script/ deletion as-is; SKILL.md hunks dropped (resolved with -X ours) in favor of #42's better rewrite. cargo test --workspace and cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings both pass.

6ae40c86-0621-4461-aaf8-eaef6d4e0e67.title: Quality pass: clippy pedantic cleanup in crossbridge-server
6ae40c86-0621-4461-aaf8-eaef6d4e0e67.description:

Run `cargo clippy -p crossbridge-server --all-targets -- -W clippy::pedantic` and resolve every warning (currently 57 — by far the largest count in the workspace). Top categories: cast_possible_truncation, missing_errors_doc, must_use_candidate, uninlined_format_args, unused_async (note: `unused_async` on `run_fake_supervisor` is a false positive — the helper hosts a future even though no `.await` is in its body; allow that one with a comment).

Acceptance criteria:
- `cargo clippy -p crossbridge-server --all-targets -- -W clippy::pedantic -D warnings` exits 0.
- No blanket `#[allow(clippy::pedantic)]` or `#[allow(...)]` over whole modules — fix the underlying issues, only allow specific lints with a one-line justification comment when the pedantic suggestion is genuinely worse.
- All existing tests still pass (`cargo test -p crossbridge-server`), including the `end_to_end` and `oversized_frame` integration tests.
- `cargo fmt --check` clean.

740c91b6-a927-4bac-89a3-26d0104e9f17.title: crossbridge-server: actionable error when slug derivation fails — point user at --slug
740c91b6-a927-4bac-89a3-26d0104e9f17.description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

## Problem

When `crossbridge-server` runs without `--slug` and the repo has no `origin` remote configured (e.g. an unpublished local repo), it bails with an unhelpful low-level error that exposes implementation details and gives the user no actionable next step.

### Repro

```
$ crossbridge-server --group orchestration
Error: deriving slug from /home/vringar/projects/vetinari

Caused by:
    `jj git remote list` did not list an `origin` remote in /home/vringar/projects/vetinari
```

The user has to read the error twice to realize the fix is "pass `--slug <name>` manually".

## Desired behavior

When slug derivation fails (no `origin` remote in either jj or git), the error should:

1. Lead with what the user must do: pass `--slug <NAME>` explicitly.
2. Mention briefly *why* derivation failed (no `origin` remote configured) so the user understands the trigger.
3. Not expose the literal `jj git remote list` / `git remote get-url origin` command string — that is an implementation detail.

A reasonable shape:

```
Error: cannot auto-derive repo slug for /home/vringar/projects/vetinari
       (the repository has no `origin` remote configured)

       Pass `--slug <NAME>` explicitly, e.g.:
           crossbridge-server --group orchestration --slug vetinari
```

## Scope

- `crossbridge-server/src/slug.rs` — change the error returned by `jj_origin_url` / `git_origin_url` when no origin remote exists, OR (preferred) keep those low-level errors but wrap them at the `derive_from_repo` boundary with the actionable message.
- `crossbridge-server/src/main.rs` — possibly adjust the `.with_context(...)` call so the surfaced top-level message names `--slug` as the fix.
- Add/update a unit test in `slug.rs` covering the "no origin remote" path if one does not already exist.

## Out of scope

- Auto-falling-back to the directory basename. The user asked for an *explicit* demand, not silent inference.
- Refactoring how slug derivation hooks into the CLI more broadly.

7bca6069-e60b-41fc-a656-f900b4eb35b0.title: Use npins to pin nixpkgs (and rustc) for deterministic local + CI builds
7bca6069-e60b-41fc-a656-f900b4eb35b0.description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

Today shell.nix uses bare `<nixpkgs>` which resolves to whatever the user's NIX_PATH points to (currently rustc 1.94). CI uses dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable which is the GitHub-hosted current stable (different rustc, different clippy, different pedantic lint set). The two diverge silently — the run on #37 fails on clippy::map_unwrap_or warnings that local 1.94 didn't flag.

Fix: introduce npins to lock a specific nixpkgs commit, refactor shell.nix to import that pin, and have CI install Nix + run cargo via nix-shell against the same shell.nix. Then pedantic-clean state is reproducible: every machine compiling this repo uses the same rustc, the same clippy, and sees the same lint set. New pedantic warnings only land when the pin is bumped, which is a deliberate act.

AC:
- npins/ directory at repo root with a nixpkgs pin (current stable channel, e.g. nixos-25.05 or unstable, whichever the user prefers).
- shell.nix imports the pinned nixpkgs instead of <nixpkgs>.
- .github/workflows/ci.yml installs Nix (cachix/install-nix-action@v27 or similar) and runs cargo test / cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -W clippy::pedantic -D warnings / cargo fmt --check inside nix-shell.
- README documents the pin: 'rustc version is whatever nixpkgs in npins/ ships'.
- The new CI run on the resulting commit passes all three jobs green.
- Local cargo build via direnv/nix-shell continues to work without manual intervention.

7f318491-f2fe-47f0-8653-94cc5b4623e4.title: Quality pass: clippy pedantic cleanup in crossbridge-e2e
7f318491-f2fe-47f0-8653-94cc5b4623e4.description:

Desired state: 

Reason: 

Run `cargo clippy -p crossbridge-e2e --all-targets -- -W clippy::pedantic` and resolve every warning (currently 57). Top categories: missing_errors_doc, cast_possible_truncation, map_unwrap_or, similar_names (`labels_a_after` vs `labels_b_after` — keep them, they're the most readable; allow `similar_names` for the e2e test specifically), too_many_lines (the round_trip test is intentionally a single linear narrative — allow `too_many_lines` on that fn with a comment), manual_assert.

Acceptance criteria:
- `cargo clippy -p crossbridge-e2e --all-targets -- -W clippy::pedantic -D warnings` exits 0.
- No blanket `#[allow(clippy::pedantic)]` or `#[allow(...)]` over whole modules — fix the underlying issues, only allow specific lints with a one-line justification comment when the pedantic suggestion is genuinely worse.
- The `submit_then_answer_round_trip` integration test must still run unconditionally (no `#[ignore]`) and pass.
- `cargo fmt --check` clean.

8a515bec-3b0e-4cac-935a-a889c8c40b52.title: Fix CI: rust 1.87 too old for transitive deps
8a515bec-3b0e-4cac-935a-a889c8c40b52.description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

First CI run on main (run 25601663135) failed because transitive deps now require rustc 1.88+:
- darling/darling_core/darling_macro 0.23.0 require 1.88
- instability 0.3.12 requires 1.88
- time/time-core require 1.88

Fix: bump workspace rust-version to 1.88 in Cargo.toml AND switch the CI workflow from a pinned 1.87 to 'stable' (so future dep bumps don't silently break CI again). Local dev shell already uses 1.94, this only catches up to reality.

AC:
- cargo build --workspace passes locally and on CI.
- .github/workflows/ci.yml uses dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable without an explicit version override.
- Cargo.toml workspace.package.rust-version is bumped (target floor that compiles).

9c3350cb-4fbf-4f2b-910c-f78718a97d98.title: crossbridge-supervisor: Implement the supervisor daemon. See .design/supervisor.md for full spec. Listens on Unix socket for repo server registrations, tracks peer groups in-memory (HashMap<String, HashMap<String, Stream>>), sends join/leave notifications over persistent streams. Single-threaded tokio event loop. Creates/removes /run/crossbridge/<slug>/ directories. Handles registration (validate unique slug), peer departure (notify survivors, cleanup dirs), and own restart (wipe state). CLI: crossbridge-supervisor [--socket path]. Dependencies: tokio, crossbridge-protocol (workspace dep), tracing, tracing-subscriber, anyhow. NO crosslink dependency.

9c3cc507-f4df-4180-904b-0f6167228f00.title: Drop rust-version from workspace Cargo.toml
9c3cc507-f4df-4180-904b-0f6167228f00.description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

rust-version was set to 1.87 in the initial skeleton commit (3cd5721) and not revisited. None of the crates are published to crates.io, and the project is only ever built from this machine's nix shell (rustc 1.94+). Each time crosslink's transitive deps bump their MSRV, CI breaks while local dev keeps working silently (run 25601663135 was today's instance, fixed by #36 by bumping to 1.88).

Drop rust-version entirely from [workspace.package] in Cargo.toml. No need for an MSRV contract on a binary-only personal project that's locked to one toolchain.

AC:
- Cargo.toml workspace.package no longer has a rust-version line.
- cargo build --workspace passes.
- CI run on the resulting commit passes (test + clippy + fmt all green).

a83c1fb3-70de-4710-9527-e19aaafc490d.title: crossbridge-client: Implement the per-agent CLI. See .design/client.md for full spec. SYNCHRONOUS (no tokio). Derives repo slug from git/jj origin remote (strip .git, take last path component). Three subcommands: (1) peers - list *.socket files in /run/crossbridge/<own-slug>/, (2) submit --issue <id> --target <slug> - read issue from local crosslink DB, connect to peer socket, send ClientRequest::Submit, on success update local labels (xb:outbound, xb-status:pending, xb-ref:<target-uuid>), (3) answer --issue <id> - read inbound issue, extract xb-source and xb-ref labels, collect result comments, send ClientRequest::Answer, on success mark xb-status:answered and close. Uses std::os::unix::net::UnixStream with sync framing helpers from crossbridge-protocol. Dependencies: crossbridge-protocol, crosslink, clap, anyhow. NO tokio.

c326e492-c0ad-4577-b092-86adbe2e3cbb.title: crossbridge-server: Implement the per-repo server. See .design/server.md for full spec. Registers with supervisor (sends Register, receives RegisterAck with peer list), creates listening sockets at /run/crossbridge/<peer-slug>/<own-slug>.socket for each peer. Handles PeerJoined/PeerLeft notifications. Accepts ClientRequest (Submit/Answer) from agents on those sockets. SubmitIssue: creates issue in local crosslink DB with proper labels, handles attachments via jj worktree materialization, idempotent via xb-ref check. SubmitAnswer: finds source issue by UUID, copies comments, swaps status to resolved, closes. Single-threaded tokio. Supervisor reconnection with exponential backoff. CLI: crossbridge-server --group <group> [--slug <slug>] [--repo-path <path>]. Dependencies: tokio, crossbridge-protocol, crosslink, tracing, tracing-subscriber, anyhow, clap.

c3dacb91-e367-4443-a3e5-e0c94bdfa066.title: Update agent skill to v2 socket protocol
c3dacb91-e367-4443-a3e5-e0c94bdfa066.description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

## Problem

`skill/crossbridge/SKILL.md` documents the v1 polling-bridge workflow, not the
shipped v2 socket-based architecture.

Concrete drift:

- Tells agents to label issues with `xb-target:<slug>` / `xb-status:open` and
  wait for "the bridge daemon" to copy them. That label-driven flow is only
  consumed by the legacy polling binary in `crossbridge/`. The v2 server does
  not poll labels — clients must call `crossbridge-client submit/answer`.
- Recommends `crossbridge-request` / `crossbridge-answer` shell helpers as the
  primary entry points. Those scripts (in `script/`) only write the v1 labels;
  under v2 they are inert.
- "Manual fallback" sections write the same v1 labels — also inert under v2.
- Footer says "the bridge runs every ~30s", which is v1 polling-cycle language.
  v2 is event-driven via Unix sockets.
- Never mentions `crossbridge-client` despite that being the only v2 entry
  point an agent can use today.

Net effect: an agent following this skill will appear to send a request but
nothing on the v2 transport will fire.

## What v2 actually wants the agent to do

- ask: `crossbridge-client submit --issue <id> --target <slug>` (after
  creating the issue with `crosslink issue create`).
- answer: `crossbridge-client answer --issue <id>` once a `result` comment
  has been added.
- check: list inbound/outbound issues by `xb:inbound` / `xb:outbound` label.
- The server appends a footer to every inbound issue's body telling the agent
  to run `crossbridge-client answer --issue <id>` (see
  `ANSWER_INSTRUCTION_FOOTER` in `crossbridge-server/src/handler.rs:23`).

## Acceptance

- `skill/crossbridge/SKILL.md` only references the v2 client commands.
- All `xb-target:` / `xb-status:open` instructions removed.
- "Manual fallback" sections either deleted or rewritten to invoke
  `crossbridge-client` directly.
- Polling-language ("every ~30s") replaced with the event-driven reality.
- Cross-check against `.design/client.md`, `.design/server.md`, and
  `crossbridge-server/src/handler.rs` so the skill matches real behavior.

## Related

- See companion issue for removal of the v1 `crossbridge/` crate; once removed,
  `script/crossbridge-request` / `script/crossbridge-answer` and any remaining
  references should be deleted as part of that work.

c6ad08b7-cf3c-465c-81f8-64e3b656dabd.title: Quality pass: clippy pedantic clean workspace-wide
c6ad08b7-cf3c-465c-81f8-64e3b656dabd.description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

Resolve every pedantic warning across the entire workspace in one coherent pass.

Why workspace-wide and not per-crate: cargo clippy -p X with -W clippy::pedantic -D warnings lints the entire dep graph, so a per-crate AC is not independently verifiable until every upstream is also clean. The previous attempt (issues #18-#23, now closed as superseded) coordinated badly across six parallel kickoffs and was abandoned.

Acceptance criteria:
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -W clippy::pedantic -D warnings exits 0.
- No blanket #[allow(clippy::pedantic)] over whole modules. Specific-lint allows must have a one-line justification comment when the pedantic suggestion is genuinely worse (e.g. similar_names on test variables that are intentionally parallel; too_many_lines on a deliberately linear narrative test; unused_async on a helper that hosts a future without a direct .await).
- All existing tests still pass: cargo test --workspace (currently 92 passed, 0 ignored, 0 failed).
- cargo fmt --check clean.
- Wire protocol behavior, public APIs, and observable test outcomes are unchanged. This is a quality pass, not a refactor — do not change message types, label schemes, or observable behavior.
- For cast-truncation lints (cast_possible_truncation, cast_sign_loss): prefer u32::try_from(...)? at boundaries; allow only where bounds are statically guaranteed (with a comment).

Reference categories from the orchestrator's pre-cleanup run (per-crate counts):
- crossbridge (v1 bin): 13 warnings (unnecessary_wraps, single_match_else, similar_names, manual_let_else)
- crossbridge-protocol: 10 (cast_possible_truncation, missing_errors_doc)
- crossbridge-supervisor: 18 (cast_possible_truncation, missing_errors_doc, semicolon_if_nothing_returned, match_wild_err_arm, used_underscore_binding)
- crossbridge-server: 57 (cast_possible_truncation, missing_errors_doc, must_use_candidate, uninlined_format_args; unused_async on run_fake_supervisor is a false positive — allow with comment)
- crossbridge-client: 13 (cast_possible_truncation, unnecessary_wraps on jj_origin_url, needless_pass_by_value, missing_errors_doc, map_unwrap_or)
- crossbridge-e2e: 57 (missing_errors_doc, cast_possible_truncation, map_unwrap_or, similar_names labels_a/labels_b — keep them and allow with comment, too_many_lines on round_trip — allow with comment, manual_assert)

Total ~168 warnings across the workspace as of the last clean main.

c7e7fa6b-7ac1-4f30-9785-323fb35260c0.title: crossbridge-protocol: Convert the project to a Cargo workspace and implement the shared protocol crate. See .design/protocol.md for the full spec. This includes: workspace Cargo.toml at root, crossbridge-protocol/ crate with all message types (Register, RegisterResponse, Notification, SupervisorMessage, ClientRequest, SubmitIssue, SubmitAnswer, AnswerComment, Attachment, ServerResponse), postcard serde, and length-prefixed framing helpers (both async with tokio AsyncRead/AsyncWrite and sync with std Read/Write). Max frame size 16 MiB. Dependencies: serde, postcard, tokio (for async helpers only). No crosslink dependency.

0008013d-207d-4b2a-9f98-3cf69fa36fd3.author: qi3w
0008013d-207d-4b2a-9f98-3cf69fa36fd3.content: Plan: Convert root to Cargo workspace with members crates/crossbridge (existing CLI) and crates/crossbridge-protocol (new library). Protocol crate defines all message types per kickoff: Register/RegisterResponse/Notification/SupervisorMessage (supervisor<->server), ClientRequest/SubmitIssue/SubmitAnswer/AnswerComment/Attachment/ServerResponse (agent<->server). postcard serde. Length-prefixed framing with u32 LE prefix and 16 MiB cap. Sync helpers using std::io::Read/Write; async helpers using tokio::io::{AsyncReadExt,AsyncWriteExt}, gated behind 'tokio' feature. No crosslink dep. Update default.nix cargoBuildFlags for workspace package selection.

61c49f3c-0dc7-42e0-9455-23a1565096a1.author: 2ZD1
61c49f3c-0dc7-42e0-9455-23a1565096a1.content:
Orchestrator decision: NOT merging the IS4n-Jeg6 attempt; keeping main's existing implementation (cf65a35 + fce0f34) as canonical for #5.

Status: #5's underlying work is in main. Proceeding to kickoff #6, #7, #8 from main as the original orchestration note (2026-05-06 17:36 comment) directed.

fa3abc10-eaac-4818-881a-031f1238412a.author: hi0b
fa3abc10-eaac-4818-881a-031f1238412a.content:
## Salvage from interrupted kickoff (agent IS4n-s15o)

A previous kickoff agent built a working draft of the protocol crate but was killed
when the outer claude-sandbox restarted, before any commits were made. The staged work
has been preserved as a patch at the repo root:

  .salvage-IS4n-s15o-protocol.patch  (888 lines, 29.8 KB)

The patch is visible to fresh kickoff worktrees because the bind mount covers the
whole project dir. Apply with:

  git apply ../../.salvage-IS4n-s15o-protocol.patch

(adjust the relative path from inside .worktrees/<your-worktree>/)

### What's in the patch

- crossbridge-protocol/Cargo.toml — serde, postcard (alloc), tokio (optional, behind `async` feature)
- crossbridge-protocol/src/lib.rs (321 lines) — all message types per .design/protocol.md:
  Register, RegisterResponse, Notification, SupervisorMessage, ClientRequest,
  SubmitIssue, SubmitAnswer, AnswerComment, Attachment, ServerResponse,
  plus ProtocolError + sync framing (write_message_sync / read_message_sync) +
  async framing (gated by `async` feature)
- crossbridge-protocol/tests/roundtrip.rs (201 lines) — sync roundtrip tests
- crossbridge-protocol/tests/async_io.rs (75 lines) — tokio async roundtrip tests
- Cargo.toml — root becomes workspace AND keeps existing crossbridge package; members = [".", "crossbridge-protocol"]
- Cargo.lock — updated for new deps
- shell.nix — adds clippy and rustfmt to nativeBuildInputs (needed for the agent_lint_commands the previous agent set up)
- src/main.rs, src/route.rs — pure `cargo fmt` artifacts, no semantic changes

### Architectural choice (different from issue spec)

The original spec says "workspace Cargo.toml at root, ... crates/crossbridge/ ...".
This salvage instead keeps src/ at the root and makes the root Cargo.toml both a
package and a workspace (members = [".", "crossbridge-protocol"]). Less churn, but
divergent from the spec. Decide whether to keep this shape or move src/ into
crates/crossbridge/ as the spec requires before applying the patch.

### Build status

- cargo build --workspace passes
- Tests not run

### Excluded from the patch (DO NOT apply)

The previous agent also modified .crosslink/hook-config.json — among other things,
it deleted the `sandbox.command` config that wraps kickoff agents in claude-sandbox.
That edit would break the sandbox isolation for subsequent kickoffs and has been
omitted from the patch. Don't recreate it. The agent_lint_commands and
agent_test_commands additions in that diff (cargo clippy / cargo fmt --check / cargo
test) are reasonable; you can re-add them by hand if useful, but make sure to keep
the sandbox.command setting intact.

ce1cc28c-59e5-400e-a3b3-a8c8925d3e19.title: Quality pass: clippy pedantic cleanup in crossbridge-supervisor
ce1cc28c-59e5-400e-a3b3-a8c8925d3e19.description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

Run `cargo clippy -p crossbridge-supervisor --all-targets -- -W clippy::pedantic` and resolve every warning (currently 18). Top categories: cast_possible_truncation, missing_errors_doc, semicolon_if_nothing_returned, match_wild_err_arm, used_underscore_binding.

Acceptance criteria:
- `cargo clippy -p crossbridge-supervisor --all-targets -- -W clippy::pedantic -D warnings` exits 0.
- No blanket `#[allow(clippy::pedantic)]` or `#[allow(...)]` over whole modules — fix the underlying issues, only allow specific lints with a one-line justification comment when the pedantic suggestion is genuinely worse.
- All existing tests still pass (`cargo test -p crossbridge-supervisor`).
- `cargo fmt --check` clean.

- cargo fmt --check clean.

- `cargo fmt --check` clean.

e52c6819-b984-4b99-a1d3-fb91671747d0.title: crossbridge-server: actionable error when slug derivation fails — point user at --slug
e52c6819-b984-4b99-a1d3-fb91671747d0.description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

## Problem

When `crossbridge-server` runs without `--slug` and the repo has no `origin` remote configured (e.g. an unpublished local repo), it bails with an unhelpful low-level error that exposes implementation details and gives the user no actionable next step.

### Repro

```
$ crossbridge-server --group orchestration
Error: deriving slug from /home/vringar/projects/vetinari

Caused by:
    `jj git remote list` did not list an `origin` remote in /home/vringar/projects/vetinari
```

The user has to read the error twice to realize the fix is "pass `--slug <name>` manually".

## Desired behavior

When slug derivation fails (no `origin` remote in either jj or git), the error should:

1. Lead with what the user must do: pass `--slug <NAME>` explicitly.
2. Mention briefly *why* derivation failed (no `origin` remote configured) so the user understands the trigger.
3. Not expose the literal `jj git remote list` / `git remote get-url origin` command string — that is an implementation detail.

A reasonable shape:

```
Error: cannot auto-derive repo slug for /home/vringar/projects/vetinari
       (the repository has no `origin` remote configured)

       Pass `--slug <NAME>` explicitly, e.g.:
           crossbridge-server --group orchestration --slug vetinari
```

## Scope

- `crossbridge-server/src/slug.rs` — change the error returned by `jj_origin_url` / `git_origin_url` when no origin remote exists, OR (preferred) keep those low-level errors but wrap them at the `derive_from_repo` boundary with the actionable message.
- `crossbridge-server/src/main.rs` — possibly adjust the `.with_context(...)` call so the surfaced top-level message names `--slug` as the fix.
- Add/update a unit test in `slug.rs` covering the "no origin remote" path if one does not already exist.

## Out of scope

- Auto-falling-back to the directory basename. The user asked for an *explicit* demand, not silent inference.
- Refactoring how slug derivation hooks into the CLI more broadly.

fc80af35-c886-4380-bbc7-b91cfcd0a0a3.title: Skill file skill/crossbridge.md is not discoverable by Claude Code
fc80af35-c886-4380-bbc7-b91cfcd0a0a3.description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

Mirror of GitHub issue: https://github.com/vringar/crossbridge/issues/1

The repo ships a Claude Code skill at skill/crossbridge.md, but in its current shape Claude Code will not pick it up. Two issues:

1. **Filename.** Claude Code skill discovery looks for SKILL.md inside a directory named after the skill, not <skill>.md at the top level of skill/. The current path needs to be skill/crossbridge/SKILL.md.
2. **Frontmatter.** Skills are technically loadable without frontmatter — Claude Code falls back to the first paragraph as the description — but in that mode the skill has no name and gets a description that wasn't written for invocation hints, so it won't be triggered reliably for relevant user messages. Skills are expected to declare YAML frontmatter with at least name and description. The current file has none.

## Suggested fix

Move skill/crossbridge.md → skill/crossbridge/SKILL.md and prepend YAML frontmatter:

```yaml
---
name: crossbridge
description: Use when sending or answering cross-project requests via crossbridge — the xb:* labeled-issue transport between crosslink repos. Covers ask, answer, and check.
---
```

See GH #1 for full discussion and rationale.
